paragraphs.source: One of the most misunderstood aspects of the Apple-TSMC relationship is the pricing model. Surface-level analysis assumes a fixed wafer price. The reality is a dynamic risk-sharing agreement that evolves over the lifecycle of a process node.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: One of the most misunderstood aspects of the Apple-TSMC relationship is the pricing model. Surface-level analysis assumes a fixed wafer price. The reality is a dynamic risk-sharing agreement that evolves over the lifecycle of a process node.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: One of the most misunderstood aspects of the Apple-TSMC relationship is the pricing model. Surface-level analysis assumes a fixed wafer price. The reality is a dynamic risk-sharing agreement that evolves over the lifecycle of a process node.

pro.text: 苹果与台积电的合作关系中，最容易被误解的一点便是定价模型。浅层分析往往假定晶圆价格固定不变。实际情况却是一项动态的风险分担协议，该协议会随着制程节点生命周期的推进而不断演变。

paragraphs.source: The Known Good Die (KGD) Model
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: ### The Known Good Die (KGD) Model
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The Known Good Die (KGD) Model

pro.text: 已知良好裸片（KGD）模式

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少必要的空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。原文中的“M系列”、“A系列”在多处（段落7、8、9、19）均未与中文字符隔开，略微影响排版规范。

paragraphs.source: When TSMC launches a bleeding-edge node (e.g., N3 for the A17 Pro), yields can be as low as 55% during the initial ramp. A standard wafer pricing model ($20,000/wafer) would be ruinous for Apple if nearly half the chips on each wafer are defective.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: When TSMC launches a bleeding-edge node (e.g., N3 for the A17 Pro), yields can be as low as 55% during the initial ramp. A standard wafer pricing model ($20,000/wafer) would be ruinous for Apple if nearly half the chips on each wafer are defective.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: When TSMC launches a bleeding-edge node (e.g., N3 for the A17 Pro), yields can be as low as 55% during the initial ramp. A standard wafer pricing model ($20,000/wafer) would be ruinous for Apple if nearly half the chips on each wafer are defective.

pro.text: 当台积电推出最尖端的制程节点（例如用于 A17 Pro 的 N3）时，在初始产能爬坡阶段，良率可能低至 55%。如果每片晶圆上近半数芯片都是次品，采用标准的晶圆定价模式（每片晶圆 20,000 美元）对苹果来说将是毁灭性的。

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少必要的空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。原文中的“M系列”、“A系列”在多处（段落7、8、9、19）均未与中文字符隔开，略微影响排版规范。
数字“20,000”直接照搬了英文的千位分隔符格式，在中文语境下阅读不够直观。

paragraphs.source: The Deal: Apple negotiates to pay only for Known Good Dies (KGD) during the initial ramp. TSMC absorbs the cost of the defects. This effectively aligns incentives: TSMC must improve yield to make money, and Apple is protected from the immaturity of the node.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The Deal: Apple negotiates to pay only for Known Good Dies (KGD) during the initial ramp. TSMC absorbs the cost of the defects. This effectively aligns incentives: TSMC must improve yield to make money, and Apple is protected from the immaturity of the node.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The Deal: Apple negotiates to pay only for Known Good Dies (KGD) during the initial ramp. TSMC absorbs the cost of the defects. This effectively aligns incentives: TSMC must improve yield to make money, and Apple is protected from the immaturity of the node.

pro.text: 交易方案：苹果通过谈判达成协议，在初始产能爬坡期间仅为已知良好裸片（KGD）买单。台积电则承担次品成本。这有效统一了双方的利益诉求：台积电必须提高良率才能盈利，而苹果也免受制程节点不成熟的拖累。

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少必要的空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。原文中的“M系列”、“A系列”在多处（段落7、8、9、19）均未与中文字符隔开，略微影响排版规范。
数字“20,000”直接照搬了英文的千位分隔符格式，在中文语境下阅读不够直观。

paragraphs.source: The Trade-off: Apple commits to enormous volumes (often buying 50-100% of initial capacity) that no other customer can match, effectively funding the R&D and depreciation of the fab.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The Trade-off: Apple commits to enormous volumes (often buying 50-100% of initial capacity) that no other customer can match, effectively funding the R&D and depreciation of the fab.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The Trade-off: Apple commits to enormous volumes (often buying 50-100% of initial capacity) that no other customer can match, effectively funding the R&D and depreciation of the fab.

pro.text: 利益交换：苹果承诺提供其他客户无法企及的庞大订单量（通常买断 50% 到 100% 的初始产能），这实际上为晶圆厂的研发和折旧提供了资金支持。

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少必要的空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。原文中的“M系列”、“A系列”在多处（段落7、8、9、19）均未与中文字符隔开，略微影响排版规范。

paragraphs.source: The Transition: Once yields hit a mature threshold, the contract reverts to standard per-wafer pricing. This incentivizes TSMC to fix yields quickly.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The Transition: Once yields hit a mature threshold, the contract reverts to standard per-wafer pricing. This incentivizes TSMC to fix yields quickly.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The Transition: Once yields hit a mature threshold, the contract reverts to standard per-wafer pricing. This incentivizes TSMC to fix yields quickly.

pro.text: 模式切换：一旦良率达到成熟阈值，合同便恢复为标准的按晶圆计价模式。这激励台积电迅速解决良率问题。

paragraphs.source: Apple utilizes its M-series product stack to maximize yield. A defective M3 Max die with a broken GPU core doesn’t go in the trash; it becomes a binned M3 Pro or a lower-tier Max.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple utilizes its M-series product stack to maximize yield. A defective M3 Max die with a broken GPU core doesn’t go in the trash; it becomes a binned M3 Pro or a lower-tier Max.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple utilizes its M-series product stack to maximize yield. A defective M3 Max die with a broken GPU core doesn’t go in the trash; it becomes a binned M3 Pro or a lower-tier Max.

pro.text: 苹果利用其 M系列产品矩阵将良率最大化。一块 GPU 核心损坏的次品 M3 Max 裸片不会被扔进垃圾桶；经过芯片分级，它会变成 M3 Pro 或低配版 Max。

paragraphs.source: A-Series: Less binning flexibility (iPhone volumes require uniformity), though the A18 vs A18 Pro distinction (5-core vs 6-core GPU) suggests aggressive binning is now entering the iPhone lineup to combat N3E yield costs. This allows Apple to use dies with a single GPU defect in the standard iPhone 16.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: A-Series: Less binning flexibility (iPhone volumes require uniformity), though the A18 vs A18 Pro distinction (5-core vs 6-core GPU) suggests aggressive binning is now entering the iPhone lineup to combat N3E yield costs. This allows Apple to use dies with a single GPU defect in the standard iPhone 16.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: A-Series: Less binning flexibility (iPhone volumes require uniformity), though the A18 vs A18 Pro distinction (5-core vs 6-core GPU) suggests aggressive binning is now entering the iPhone lineup to combat N3E yield costs. This allows Apple to use dies with a single GPU defect in the standard iPhone 16.

pro.text: A系列：芯片分级灵活性较低（iPhone 的庞大出货量要求芯片具备高度一致性）。不过，A18 与 A18 Pro 的差异（5 核与 6 核 GPU）表明，激进的芯片分级策略正引入 iPhone 产品线，以对冲 N3E 的良率成本。这使得苹果能在标准版 iPhone 16 中使用存在单个 GPU 缺陷的裸片。

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少必要的空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。原文中的“M系列”、“A系列”在多处（段落7、8、9、19）均未与中文字符隔开，略微影响排版规范。

paragraphs.source: M-Series: Extreme binning. M3 Max exists in 30-core and 40-core GPU variants. This allows Apple to harvest dies that would otherwise yield losses. This strategy is crucial for maintaining margins on large, expensive dies like the M-series Max and Ultra.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: M-Series: Extreme binning. M3 Max exists in 30-core and 40-core GPU variants. This allows Apple to harvest dies that would otherwise yield losses. This strategy is crucial for maintaining margins on large, expensive dies like the M-series Max and Ultra.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: M-Series: Extreme binning. M3 Max exists in 30-core and 40-core GPU variants. This allows Apple to harvest dies that would otherwise yield losses. This strategy is crucial for maintaining margins on large, expensive dies like the M-series Max and Ultra.

pro.text: M系列：极致的芯片分级。M3 Max 拥有 30 核与 40 核 GPU 两个版本。这使得苹果能够回收利用那些原本会作为良率损耗报废的裸片。对于 M系列 Max 和 Ultra 这种大面积、高成本的裸片而言，该策略是维持利润率的关键。

paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: In 2020, shortly after Apple commenced 5nm volume production for the iPhone 12 (A14 chip), Fab 18 reported having just 4 customers. This exclusivity explains how Apple could secure 100% of the initial capacity.

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少必要的空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。原文中的“M系列”、“A系列”在多处（段落7、8、9、19）均未与中文字符隔开，略微影响排版规范。

paragraphs.source: TSMC’s capex trajectory reveals three distinct eras. Pre-Apple (2005-2009), annual spending averaged $2.4B. The foundry had no anchor tenant and limited visibility into future demand. Post-Apple (2010-2018), capex climbed to $8B annually as A-series volumes justified larger bets. The EUV era (2019-2022) broke the model entirely. TSMC deployed $98B in four years, exceeding the cumulative $92B spent over the prior 14 years. This acceleration required certainty. Apple’s volume commitments and manufacturing purchase obligations gave TSMC the confidence to make these bets. Without a guaranteed buyer for bleeding-edge nodes, no foundry can justify $30B+ annual capex.

The EUV era (2019-2022) broke the model entirely. TSMC deployed $98B in four years, exceeding the cumulative $92B spent over the prior 14 years. This acceleration required certainty. Apple’s volume commitments and manufacturing purchase obligations gave TSMC the confidence to make these bets. Without a guaranteed buyer for bleeding-edge nodes, no foundry can justify $30B+ annual capex.

Apple’s volume commitments and manufacturing purchase obligations gave TSMC the confidence to make these bets. Without a guaranteed buyer for bleeding-edge nodes, no foundry can justify $30B+ annual capex.

pro.text: 台积电的资本支出轨迹呈现出三个截然不同的时代。前苹果时代（2005-2009 年），年均支出仅 24 亿美元。当时这家晶圆代工厂缺乏锚定客户，对未来需求的可见度也十分有限。后苹果时代（2010-2018 年），A系列芯片的庞大出货量为更大规模的投资提供了依据，资本支出攀升至每年 80 亿美元。EUV 时代（2019-2022 年）彻底打破了这一模式。台积电在四年内狂砸 980 亿美元，超过了此前 14 年累计支出的 920 亿美元。这种加速扩张需要极高的确定性。正是苹果的产量承诺与制造采购承诺，赋予了台积电重金下注的底气。如果尖端制程节点没有保底买家，任何晶圆代工厂都无法支撑起每年超 300 亿美元的资本支出。

paragraphs.source: Apple has effectively financed $73B of TSMC’s leading-edge capacity since the A8 launched on 20nm in 2014, representing 47% of all advanced node capex over that period. This is 1.7x the $43B implied by Apple’s 22-25% revenue share. The premium stems from Apple’s role as sole anchor tenant at node launch: near 100% of 20nm in 2014 and 10nm in 2017, and over 90% of N3 in 2023.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple has effectively financed $73B of TSMC’s leading-edge capacity since the A8 launched on 20nm in 2014, representing 47% of all advanced node capex over that period. This is 1.7x the $43B implied by Apple’s 22-25% revenue share. The premium stems from Apple’s role as sole anchor tenant at node launch: near 100% of 20nm in 2014 and 10nm in 2017, and over 90% of N3 in 2023.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple has effectively financed $73B of TSMC’s leading-edge capacity since the A8 launched on 20nm in 2014, representing 47% of all advanced node capex over that period. This is 1.7x the $43B implied by Apple’s 22-25% revenue share. The premium stems from Apple’s role as sole anchor tenant at node launch: near 100% of 20nm in 2014 and 10nm in 2017, and over 90% of N3 in 2023.

pro.text: 自 2014 年 A8 芯片在 20 纳米制程节点发布以来，苹果实质上为台积电 730 亿美元的先进制程产能提供了资金支持，占同期所有先进制程节点资本支出的 47%。按照苹果 22% 至 25% 的营收占比推算，其对应的资金规模应为 430 亿美元，而实际出资额是这一数字的 1.7 倍。这种溢价源于苹果在节点发布初期的独家锚定客户地位：2014 年的 20 纳米和 2017 年的 10 纳米节点，苹果几乎包揽了 100% 的产能；2023 年的 N3 节点，苹果也吃下了超 90% 的产能。

paragraphs.source: These figures use lifetime utilization weighting, crediting Apple only for its share of each node’s output over a 5-year depreciation cycle rather than assigning full launch-year capex. Even under this conservative methodology, Apple’s contribution dwarfs all other customers combined. TSMC’s cumulative $259B capex through 2025 translates to $83B in Apple-attributed infrastructure, exceeding Apple’s own semiconductor R&D spend over the same period.

TSMC’s cumulative $259B capex through 2025 translates to $83B in Apple-attributed infrastructure, exceeding Apple’s own semiconductor R&D spend over the same period.

pro.text: 上述数据采用了全生命周期利用率加权算法。该算法并未将节点发布首年的资本支出全额记在苹果账上，而是按 5 年折旧周期内苹果在该节点总产量中的份额进行折算。即使采用这种保守的计算方法，苹果的贡献依然令所有其他客户的总和相形见绌。截至 2025 年，台积电累计 2590 亿美元的资本支出中，有 830 亿美元的基础设施投资可归功于苹果。这一数字甚至超过了苹果同期自身的半导体研发支出。

paragraphs.source: Qualcomm, AMD, NVIDIA, and MediaTek collectively account for 25% of TSMC revenue but bear roughly 30% of leading-edge capex burden because they backfill capacity 12-18 months after Apple has absorbed yield risk and funded the initial ramp. The dynamic creates mutual lock-in: Apple gets first access to density and efficiency gains, TSMC gets a guaranteed anchor tenant to de-risk $25-30B annual node transitions.

The dynamic creates mutual lock-in: Apple gets first access to density and efficiency gains, TSMC gets a guaranteed anchor tenant to de-risk $25-30B annual node transitions.

The dynamic creates mutual lock-in: Apple gets first access to density and efficiency gains, TSMC gets a guaranteed anchor tenant to de-risk $25-30B annual node transitions.

pro.text: 高通、AMD、英伟达和联发科合计贡献了台积电 25% 的营收，却承担了约 30% 的先进制程资本支出重担。这是因为它们通常在苹果消化了良率风险并为初期产能爬坡提供资金的 12 到 18 个月后，才来填补产能空缺。这种动态机制促成了双向绑定：苹果得以率先享受晶体管密度和效率提升的红利；台积电则获得了一个有保障的锚定客户，从而化解每年高达 250 亿至 300 亿美元制程节点迭代的巨额风险。

paragraphs.source: TSMC’s R&D spending has grown 8x since 2010, from under $1B to $8B in 2025. But R&D intensity tells a different story. In the 2015-2019 period when Apple was the primary driver of leading-edge scaling, TSMC consistently spent 8%+ of revenue on R&D. That ratio has since dropped to under 7%, not because TSMC is investing less, but because AI-driven revenue is scaling faster than the R&D required to support it. Apple funded the hard yards of EUV transition and sub-7nm development; AI customers are now harvesting the returns on that investment at scale.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: TSMC’s R&D spending has grown 8x since 2010, from under $1B to $8B in 2025. But R&D intensity tells a different story. In the 2015-2019 period when Apple was the primary driver of leading-edge scaling, TSMC consistently spent 8%+ of revenue on R&D. That ratio has since dropped to under 7%, not because TSMC is investing less, but because AI-driven revenue is scaling faster than the R&D required to support it. Apple funded the hard yards of EUV transition and sub-7nm development; AI customers are now harvesting the returns on that investment at scale.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: TSMC’s R&D spending has grown 8x since 2010, from under $1B to $8B in 2025. But R&D intensity tells a different story. In the 2015-2019 period when Apple was the primary driver of leading-edge scaling, TSMC consistently spent 8%+ of revenue on R&D. That ratio has since dropped to under 7%, not because TSMC is investing less, but because AI-driven revenue is scaling faster than the R&D required to support it. Apple funded the hard yards of EUV transition and sub-7nm development; AI customers are now harvesting the returns on that investment at scale.

pro.text: 自 2010 年以来，台积电的研发支出增长了 8 倍，从不足 10 亿美元攀升至 2025 年的 80 亿美元。但研发强度却呈现出另一番景象。在 2015 至 2019 年间，当苹果还是推动先进制程演进的主力时，台积电的研发支出始终保持在营收的 8% 以上。如今该比例已降至 7% 以下，这并非因为台积电缩减了投资，而是因为 AI 驱动的营收增速，远超支撑其所需的研发投入增速。苹果出资扛过了 EUV 转型和 7 纳米以下制程开发的攻坚阶段；而 AI 客户如今正在大规模收割这些投资的红利。

paragraphs.source: TSMC’s customer concentration has steadily increased over the past two decades, with the top-10 customers growing from 44% of revenue in 2000 to 78% by 2025. Apple has been a primary driver of this concentration. Before 2014, Apple was a negligible contributor to TSMC’s revenue; by 2025, Apple alone represents roughly a quarter of top-10 customer revenue. For most of the 2014-2022 period, Apple grew faster than the average of the other nine top-10 customers, pulling TSMC’s customer mix increasingly toward Cupertino.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: TSMC’s customer concentration has steadily increased over the past two decades, with the top-10 customers growing from 44% of revenue in 2000 to 78% by 2025. Apple has been a primary driver of this concentration. Before 2014, Apple was a negligible contributor to TSMC’s revenue; by 2025, Apple alone represents roughly a quarter of top-10 customer revenue. For most of the 2014-2022 period, Apple grew faster than the average of the other nine top-10 customers, pulling TSMC’s customer mix increasingly toward Cupertino.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: TSMC’s customer concentration has steadily increased over the past two decades, with the top-10 customers growing from 44% of revenue in 2000 to 78% by 2025. Apple has been a primary driver of this concentration. Before 2014, Apple was a negligible contributor to TSMC’s revenue; by 2025, Apple alone represents roughly a quarter of top-10 customer revenue. For most of the 2014-2022 period, Apple grew faster than the average of the other nine top-10 customers, pulling TSMC’s customer mix increasingly toward Cupertino.

pro.text: 过去二十年间，台积电的客户集中度稳步攀升，前十大客户的营收占比从 2000 年的 44% 增长至 2025 年的 78%。苹果一直是推动这一集中度上升的核心动力。2014 年之前，苹果对台积电的营收贡献微乎其微；而到 2025 年，仅苹果一家就占据了前十大客户营收的约四分之一。在 2014 至 2022 年的大部分时间里，苹果的增速超过了前十大客户中其余九家的平均水平，使得台积电的客户结构日益向库比蒂诺（译注：苹果总部）倾斜。

paragraphs.source: That dynamic has now reversed. In 2024 and 2025, non-Apple revenue at TSMC grew at nearly double Apple’s rate, driven by AI accelerator demand from NVIDIA, Broadcom, AMD, and hyperscaler custom silicon. The ex-Apple top-10 cohort, which grew at single-digit rates for much of the 2010s, is now expanding at 40%+ annually. Apple remains TSMC’s largest single customer, but its growth is being outpaced by a coalition of AI-focused buyers who are collectively reshaping the foundry’s revenue base. The customer concentration continues to rise, but the center of gravity is shifting away from smartphones and toward data center silicon.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: That dynamic has now reversed. In 2024 and 2025, non-Apple revenue at TSMC grew at nearly double Apple’s rate, driven by AI accelerator demand from NVIDIA, Broadcom, AMD, and hyperscaler custom silicon. The ex-Apple top-10 cohort, which grew at single-digit rates for much of the 2010s, is now expanding at 40%+ annually. Apple remains TSMC’s largest single customer, but its growth is being outpaced by a coalition of AI-focused buyers who are collectively reshaping the foundry’s revenue base. The customer concentration continues to rise, but the center of gravity is shifting away from smartphones and toward data center silicon.

In 2024 and 2025, non-Apple revenue at TSMC grew at nearly double Apple’s rate, driven by AI accelerator demand from NVIDIA, Broadcom, AMD, and hyperscaler custom silicon. The ex-Apple top-10 cohort, which grew at single-digit rates for much of the 2010s, is now expanding at 40%+ annually. Apple remains TSMC’s largest single customer, but its growth is being outpaced by a coalition of AI-focused buyers who are collectively reshaping the foundry’s revenue base. The customer concentration continues to rise, but the center of gravity is shifting away from smartphones and toward data center silicon.

pro.text: 如今，这种动态已经逆转。在 2024 和 2025 年，受英伟达、博通、AMD 的 AI 加速器以及超大规模云服务商定制芯片的需求驱动，台积电非苹果业务的营收增速几乎是苹果的两倍。在 2010 年代的大部分时间里，除苹果外的前十大客户群体的增速仅为个位数，而如今正以每年 40% 以上的速度扩张。苹果依然是台积电最大的单一客户，但其增速已被一个由 AI 核心买家组成的联盟所超越，这些买家正在合力重塑这家晶圆代工厂的营收基本盘。客户集中度仍在持续攀升，但重心正从智能手机向数据中心芯片转移。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s lead is shrinking. In 2020, Apple’s revenue was 4.6x the average of the other top-9 customers. By 2025, that ratio has compressed to 2.9x. Apple is still the largest customer by far, but the gap is closing as AI customers scale.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple’s lead is shrinking. In 2020, Apple’s revenue was 4.6x the average of the other top-9 customers. By 2025, that ratio has compressed to 2.9x. Apple is still the largest customer by far, but the gap is closing as AI customers scale.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple’s lead is shrinking. In 2020, Apple’s revenue was 4.6x the average of the other top-9 customers. By 2025, that ratio has compressed to 2.9x. Apple is still the largest customer by far, but the gap is closing as AI customers scale.

pro.text: 苹果的领先优势正在缩小。2020 年，苹果贡献的营收是其余九大客户平均值的 4.6 倍。到 2025 年，这一比例已压缩至 2.9 倍。苹果目前依然是遥遥领先的最大客户，但随着 AI 客户规模的不断扩大，这一差距正在不断缩小。

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少必要的空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。原文中的“M系列”、“A系列”在多处（段落7、8、9、19）均未与中文字符隔开，略微影响排版规范。
数字“20,000”直接照搬了英文的千位分隔符格式，在中文语境下阅读不够直观。

paragraphs.source: The 2022 dip is notable. Top-10 concentration actually fell from 74% in 2020 to 68% in 2022 before rebounding. This coincided with the crypto winter and memory correction, which hit several top-10 customers hard. The rebound to 78% by 2025 is AI-driven.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The 2022 dip is notable. Top-10 concentration actually fell from 74% in 2020 to 68% in 2022 before rebounding. This coincided with the crypto winter and memory correction, which hit several top-10 customers hard. The rebound to 78% by 2025 is AI-driven.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The 2022 dip is notable. Top-10 concentration actually fell from 74% in 2020 to 68% in 2022 before rebounding. This coincided with the crypto winter and memory correction, which hit several top-10 customers hard. The rebound to 78% by 2025 is AI-driven.

pro.text: 2022 年的回落值得注意。前十大客户集中度实际上从 2020 年的 74% 降至 2022 年的 68%，随后才触底反弹。这恰逢加密货币寒冬与存储市场回调，重创了数家前十大客户。到 2025 年集中度回升至 78%，则是受 AI 驱动。

paragraphs.source: Ex-Apple top-10 growth inflected. From 2014-2021, the ex-Apple top-10 cohort grew at single-digit rates most years. In 2023, that flipped to +37%. In 2024, +40%. The acceleration is almost entirely NVIDIA and hyperscaler ASICs.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Ex-Apple top-10 growth inflected. From 2014-2021, the ex-Apple top-10 cohort grew at single-digit rates most years. In 2023, that flipped to +37%. In 2024, +40%. The acceleration is almost entirely NVIDIA and hyperscaler ASICs.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Ex-Apple top-10 growth inflected. From 2014-2021, the ex-Apple top-10 cohort grew at single-digit rates most years. In 2023, that flipped to +37%. In 2024, +40%. The acceleration is almost entirely NVIDIA and hyperscaler ASICs.

pro.text: 剔除苹果后的前十大客户增速迎来拐点。2014 至 2021 年间，这一群体的营收增速在多数年份仅为个位数。2023 年，该数字逆转跃升至 +37%。2024 年进一步达到 +40%。这种加速几乎完全归功于英伟达和超大规模云服务商的 ASIC 芯片。

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少必要的空格（CJK-Latin spacing）。原文中的“M系列”、“A系列”在多处（段落7、8、9、19）均未与中文字符隔开，略微影响排版规范。

paragraphs.source: Concentration is a double-edged sword. With 78% of revenue coming from 10 customers, TSMC is increasingly dependent on a small number of buyers. Apple and NVIDIA alone likely represent 40%+ of total revenue by 2025. Any demand shock from either would be material.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Concentration is a double-edged sword. With 78% of revenue coming from 10 customers, TSMC is increasingly dependent on a small number of buyers. Apple and NVIDIA alone likely represent 40%+ of total revenue by 2025. Any demand shock from either would be material.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Concentration is a double-edged sword. With 78% of revenue coming from 10 customers, TSMC is increasingly dependent on a small number of buyers. Apple and NVIDIA alone likely represent 40%+ of total revenue by 2025. Any demand shock from either would be material.

pro.text: 客户集中度是一把双刃剑。台积电 78% 的营收来自 10 家客户，对少数买家的依赖愈发显著。到 2025 年，仅苹果和英伟达两家就可能占据总营收的 40% 以上。这两家公司中任何一方的需求震荡，都将产生重大影响。
